title: packera subnuda var. subnuda
intro: Packera subnuda var. subnuda, also known as narrowleaf ragwort or mountain ragwort, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Packera subnuda var. subnuda include reducing inflammation and promoting healing, as well as acting as a natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
